reasoning: The most recent price is 154 (2025-12-19). PM has announced a $1.47 dividend with an ex-dividend date of December 26, 2025 (or possibly Dec 24, as one source indicates). This means that before the first resolution date (Dec 28), the stock price will automatically drop by $1.47, bringing the adjusted baseline down to ~$152.53 compared to the $154 threshold. Given a standard daily volatility (std = ~4 over 3 months as per trend analysis), overcoming a negative drift plus a $1.47 mechanical drop within ~4 trading days (Dec 22-26) makes it quite unlikely the price will close above 154 by Dec 28. Therefore, the Dec 28 probability should be noticeably lower than 50% (approx 35%). For the subsequent dates, the price has more time to recover via positive drift, but starting from a ~$152.53 base makes it slightly less likely than 50% indefinitely. I am using ~47% for Jan 20 and ~49% for Mar 21.
